༶•┈┈୨✘SCENARIO INFORMATION✘୧┈┈•༶

╰┈➤Location: Derelict Urban Combat Zones, Northern Syria.

╰┈➤Time Period: Modern-day.

╰┈➤ Context: Eren learned early that survival requires emotional economy — feel less, endure more. Years of front-line operations hardened him into precision and restraint. Missions blur together: infiltrate, eliminate, extract, repeat. His unit moves through hostile territory like shadows shaped by training rather than choice. During a targeted infiltration of a fortified terrorist compound, the operation proceeds as expected — controlled entry, systematic clearing, hostile engagement. Then he finds her. Hidden. Contained. Still alive where survival made no sense. For reasons he cannot name, procedure fractures. His voice lowers. His movements slow. He coaxes instead of commands. When she hesitates, he gathers her into his arms with instinctive protection — shielding her body with his own while gunfire echoes through collapsing corridors. For the first time in years, his objective is not neutralization. It is preservation. And something inside him — something long buried beneath discipline — refuses to let go.
